edges.explanation: Ilya Sutskever discussed a plan with Greg Brockman to remove Elon Musk from OpenAI's board.
edges.provenance: Brockman and Sutskever discussed a plan to kick Elon off OpenAI’s board in order to move forward

edges.explanation: Greg Brockman's journal entries reflected on the 'nasty fight' and moral implications of excluding Elon Musk from OpenAI's for-profit conversion.
edges.provenance: ‘[C]an’t see us turning this into a for-profit without a very nasty fight,” Brockman wrote. “[I’m] just thinking about the office and we’re in the office. and his story will correctly be that we weren’t honest with him in the end about still wanting to do the for profit just without him….btw another realization from this is that it’d be wrong to steal the non-profit from him. to convert to a b-corp without him. that’d be pretty morally bankrupt. and he’s really not an idiot.”

edges.explanation: Greg Brockman wrote in his journal about wanting to 'get out from Elon' and questioned if Musk was the right leader.
edges.provenance: “This is the only chance we have to get out from Elon,” he wrote during the talks. “Is he the ‘glorious leader’ that I would pick?

edges.explanation: Steve Molo, Elon Musk's main trial attorney, berated Greg Brockman during the trial.
edges.provenance: which became an opportunity for Steve Molo, the main trial attorney for Musk, to berate him.

edges.explanation: Steve Molo is the main trial attorney representing Elon Musk.
edges.provenance: Steve Molo, the main trial attorney for Musk

edges.explanation: Steve Molo questioned Greg Brockman about his personal wealth and donations to charity.
edges.provenance: “Why didn’t you take the $29 billion more than the billion you said you would be good with, and donate that to the charity?” Molo demanded.

edges.explanation: Steve Molo highlighted that Greg Brockman had stated he would donate to OpenAI but never did.
edges.provenance: Molo also dwelt on emails from where Brockman said he will donate $100,000 to OpenAI, something he never did.

edges.explanation: Greg Brockman made a large political donation to a SuperPAC supporting President Donald Trump.
edges.provenance: Brockman might be best known to the public for making the largest donation of the 2025 political cycle, $25 million given to MAGA Inc., a SuperPAC supporting President Donald Trump

edges.explanation: Steve Molo mocked Greg Brockman's description of a tense meeting with Elon Musk.
edges.provenance: Molo did mock Brockman’s description of the charged meeting around his control of the company as Musk being “mean” to Brockman

edges.explanation: Steve Molo suggested that Elon Musk understood governance issues better than Greg Brockman.
edges.provenance: suggested that Brockman didn’t understand the governance issues the way Musk, a serial founder, did.

edges.explanation: Greg Brockman testified that Elon Musk did not and does not understand AI.
edges.provenance: Brockman, though, said Musk didn’t understand AI. “He did not and does not know AI,” he testified

edges.explanation: Greg Brockman criticized Elon Musk for failing to recognize the potential of early AI research.
edges.provenance: The fact that Elon saw this very early version of the research, that really set all these things in motion, [and] didn’t recognize that spark — that was exactly the kind of thing that was critical to avoid happening in this environment,” Brockman said.

edges.explanation: Elon Musk suspected that Sam Altman and Greg Brockman had outmaneuvered him.
edges.provenance: Musk’s suspicions that Altman and Brockman got one over on him, leading him to file his suit in 2024.

edges.explanation: Elon Musk suspected that Sam Altman and Greg Brockman had outmaneuvered him.
edges.provenance: Musk’s suspicions that Altman and Brockman got one over on him, leading him to file his suit in 2024.
